AgentConfig.description:
Agent integration configuration.

Describes how the tool can adapt its output for AI agents — which output
modes it supports, what flag enables machine-readable output, and any
environment variables the tool recognizes for agent-aware behavior.

default_output_flag.description: Default flag to get machine-readable output (e.g. `"--json"`).

env_vars.description:
Environment variables the tool recognizes for agent behavior.
Keys are var names, values are descriptions of what setting them does.

constraints.description: Validation constraints (e.g. min/max length, regex pattern).

default.description: Default value when not provided, serialized as a JSON value.

Command.description:
A single CLI command definition.

Contains everything the bridge needs to generate a correct MCP tool:
what the command does, what it takes, what it returns, whether it's safe,
and what can go wrong.

agent_description.description:
Optional AI-facing description override. When present, the bridge
uses this in tool definitions instead of `description`. Useful when
the agent needs different context than a human.

args.description: Positional and named arguments this command accepts.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this command does.

destructive.description:
Whether this command is irreversible or dangerous.
Implies `mutating: true` — the validation layer (issue #5) enforces
this invariant: `destructive: true, mutating: false` is invalid.

errors.description: Error kinds this command can produce (references global error `kind`s).

examples.description: Worked invocation examples. Emitted at Tier 2. Optional.

interactive.description: Whether this command requires interactive input (TTY, browser, etc.).

mutating.description:
Whether this command changes state. `false` means read-only and
safe to call speculatively.

non_interactive_alternative.description:
If `interactive` is true, an alternative invocation that works
non-interactively (e.g. `"gh auth login --with-token < token_file"`).

output.description: Schema describing the command's output shape.

prerequisites.description: Commands or conditions that must be satisfied before invoking.

ContextRequirements.description:
Runtime context requirements.

Hints about what the runtime environment must provide for this tool to
function. The bridge can use these to skip tools that won't work in the
current context (e.g. offline, no git repo).

requires_auth.description: Whether the tool requires authentication to be set up first.

requires_elevated.description: Whether the tool requires elevated/root permissions.

requires_git_repo.description: Whether the tool must be run inside a git repository.

requires_network.description: Whether the tool requires network access.

typical_environment.description:
Free-form description of the typical environment (e.g. "any", "ci",
"developer workstation").

ErrorDef.description:
A global error definition.

Errors are defined at the manifest level and referenced by kind from
individual commands. This avoids repeating the same error descriptions
across commands and gives agents a consistent error taxonomy.

description.description: Human-readable description of the error condition.

retryable.description: Whether this error is transient and worth retrying.

Example.description:
A worked invocation example for a command.

Examples are the single highest-value signal for an agent deciding how to
call a command: they show a concrete, assembled invocation and (optionally)
what a successful result looks like. Emitted at Tier 2.

description.description: What this example demonstrates (e.g. "Check a PR's merge readiness").

invocation.description:
The concrete invocation, as the agent would run it. Includes the
base_command prefix so it is copy-runnable.

note.description: Optional note on when to use this form (caveats, preconditions).

output.description:
Optional illustrative output. A JSON value when the command emits
structured output; the bridge may truncate large samples.

default.description: Default value when not provided, serialized as a JSON value.

OutputField.description: A single field within an output schema.

OutputSchema.description:
Schema describing a command's output shape.

Agents need to know the return structure before calling a command so they
can plan how to parse and use the result.

When `output_type` is `"object"`, `fields` describes the object's
properties. When `"array"`, `items` describes the schema of each
element (which itself can have `fields` if items are objects).

fields.description: Fields within the output (used when `output_type` is `"object"`).

items.description: For array types, the schema of each item in the array.

Pathway.description:
A common workflow encoded as a sequence of steps.

Pathways capture expert knowledge about how to accomplish a task with the
tool. Instead of the agent discovering through trial and error, the
manifest declares the optimal sequence.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this pathway accomplishes.

prerequisites.description: Commands or conditions that must be satisfied before starting.

PathwayArg.description: A single argument token within a pathway step, in invocation order.

oneOf.description: A positional argument. Rendered as its value: `<KEY>`, `5`.
A flag/option. Rendered as `--count 5`, or bare `--json` when value is None.

PathwayStep.description: A single step within a pathway.

args.description:
Arguments to pass, in invocation order. Preserving order matters
because positional arguments are rendered by position and flags by
the sequence the author intended.

command.description: Command name to invoke (must exist in the manifest's `commands` map).

note.description: Optional human-readable note explaining this step's purpose.

Tiers.description:
Deferred loading tiers.

The bridge uses these to decide which commands to include at each
projection tier. `core` commands are loaded when the agent engages with
the tool, `common` commands are available but not pre-loaded, and
`extended` commands are loaded only on demand.

common.description: Frequently used commands — available but not pre-loaded.

core.description: Most important commands — loaded at Tier 1.

extended.description: Specialized commands — loaded only on demand (Tier 2).

description:
Top-level manifest describing a CLI tool.

This is the canonical representation that tool authors write and that the
tsukai bridge reads to generate MCP tool definitions. It contains everything
needed to understand a tool's commands, arguments, output shapes, mutation
semantics, error taxonomy, and recommended workflows.

# Forward Compatibility

This struct intentionally does **not** use `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]`.
Older parsers reading a newer manifest version will silently ignore fields
they don't recognize rather than failing to deserialize. This is a deliberate
design choice — not an omission — so that manifest authors can adopt new
schema features without breaking consumers that haven't upgraded yet.

$schema.description:
JSON Schema URI for this manifest version (e.g.
`"https://tsukai-mcp.github.io/tsukai-static/manifest/v1.json"`).

agent.description: Agent integration configuration (output modes, env vars).

base_command.description:
Base command prefix. For tools invoked as `binary subcommand` (e.g.
`mx kv`), this holds the full prefix so individual command entries
only need to specify the leaf name.

commands.description:
Command definitions, keyed by command name.

Keys use dot notation for subcommands (e.g. `"pr.view"`,
`"memory.search"`). This keeps the map flat regardless of nesting
depth. The bridge reconstructs the command tree from dots when
generating Tier 0 group projections.

context.description: Runtime context requirements (network, auth, git repo, etc.).

description.description: One-line description of what this tool does.

errors.description: Global error taxonomy. Individual commands reference these by `kind`.

pathways.description: Common workflows encoded as step-by-step pathways.

tiers.description:
Deferred loading tiers — which commands belong to core, common, or
extended tiers for the bridge's context budget management.

version.description:
Tool version (the version of the CLI being described), following semver.
The manifest schema version is encoded in the `$schema` URL.

title: Manifest
